title: Property, housing and estate managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations. Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization, such as records and information management, mail distribution, and other office support services. (Based on 44 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Present purchase offers to sellers for consideration and act as an intermediary in negotiations between buyers and sellers.
Accompany buyers during visits to property, advising them on suitability and value, and inspect condition of premises for necessary maintenance.
Answer clients' questions regarding construction work, financing, maintenance, repairs, and appraisals; review plans for new construction with clients and recommend options.
Coordinate appointments to show homes, assist buyers in comparing properties to determine competitive market price, and generate lists of compatible properties.
Promote sales of properties through advertisements, open houses, and participation in multiple listing services; coordinate property closings.
Prepare documents such as representation contracts, purchase agreements, and closing statements while maintaining knowledge of real estate laws and financing options.
Advise sellers on how to make homes appealing to potential buyers, and advise clients on market conditions, prices, legal requirements, and financing.
Appraise property values and assess income potential while managing operations and improvements of properties.
Inspect grounds and facilities routinely to determine the necessity of repairs or maintenance; investigate complaints and resolve problems following management rules.
Meet with clients to negotiate management contracts, determine priorities and discuss financial status, while establishing relationships with other organizations.
Direct fundraising activities and oversee program budgets while representing organizations in relations with governmental and media institutions.
Recruit, interview, and hire volunteers and staff, while implementing training programs and evaluating their performance.
Plan and coordinate general maintenance and major repairs for properties, and ensure compliance with existing regulations.
Generate lists of properties for sale, their locations and descriptions, while keeping abreast of real estate markets through ongoing research.
Conduct property evaluations, research community needs, and advise on program requirements related to property management.
Maintain awareness of current tax regulations and zoning laws that impact property management and operations.
Market vacant space to prospective tenants; solicit and compile listings of available rental properties.
Inspect properties before showing to clients and coordinate service contracts for cleaning and maintenance.
Monitor the fulfillment of purchase contract terms and ensure compliance with real estate laws and regulations.
Contact previous clients for prospecting referral business and follow up on inquiries regarding property sales.
Confer with legal authorities to ensure compliance with nondiscriminatory renting and advertising practices.
Prepare budgets and financial reports for properties and develop networks of professionals in the real estate industry for client referrals.
Coordinate security operations or activities with law enforcement and fire agencies.
Conduct threat or vulnerability analyses to determine potential impacts of disasters or criminal activity on the organization.
Conduct physical examinations of property to ensure compliance with security policies and regulations.
Communicate security status and potential problems using established protocols.
Plan, administer, and control budgets for contracts, equipment, and supplies.
Participate in architectural and engineering planning and design, including space management.
Oversee the maintenance and repair of machinery and electrical systems.
Assess risks to mitigate potential consequences of incidents and develop response plans.
Prepare and review operational reports to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
Oversee construction projects to ensure compliance with health, security, and government regulations.
Write or review security documents such as incident reports and proposals.
Direct emergency management and contingency planning activities.
Develop or manage security procedures for operations such as access control and reporting tools.
Develop, implement, or evaluate policies to protect personnel against harassment or threats.
Monitor compliance with security policies and procedures to meet internal and government requirements.
Plan and coordinate security for special events to safeguard attendees.
Evaluate the performance of vendors and service providers for quality and cost-efficiency.
Train security professionals in rules, policies, and procedures.
Attend seminars or conferences to stay updated on legislative changes or new technologies impacting security operations.
Monitor forecasts and quotas to identify changes and predict effects on supply chain activities.
Book transportation and hotel reservations, using computer or telephone.
Provide customers with brochures and publications containing travel information, such as local customs or points of interest.
Prepare, maintain, or update security procedures and related documentation.
Train personnel in security procedures or the use of security equipment.
Respond to emergency situations on an on-call basis and ensure communication of key risks.
Develop or implement risk-assessment models and recommend ways to control or reduce risk.
Conduct security audits to identify potential vulnerabilities and assess or propose sustainability initiatives.
Prepare reports summarizing operational results, financial performance, or accomplishments of specified objectives.
Collaborate with department teams to develop sustainability project goals, objectives, or strategies.
Keep records pertaining to purchases, sales, and requisitions, and monitor sales staff performance to ensure goals are met.
Explain rules and regulations of facilities and entertainment attractions to customers.
Develop and maintain partnerships with law enforcement agencies or loss prevention community members.
Conduct internal investigations of issues like employee theft and violations of loss prevention policies.
Collaborate with law enforcement to investigate and solve theft or fraud cases.
Assess security needs across locations to ensure proper deployment of loss prevention resources.
Analyze retail data to identify trends in theft or fraud.
Train staff on loss control and prevention measures.
Supervise surveillance and criminal processing related to theft cases.
Calculate and record department expenses and revenue.
Verify correct use and maintenance of physical security systems.
Visit stores to ensure compliance with company policies and procedures.
Direct installation of security equipment, like surveillance cameras.
Conduct interviews or investigations of individuals suspected of shoplifting.
Monitor compliance with operational, safety, or inventory control procedures, including security standards.
Monitor regulatory compliance to ensure effective resolution of complaints.
Recruit, interview, or hire employees to fill staff vacancies.
Respond to customer inquiries or complaints regarding services rendered.
Develop or implement marketing strategies to promote wellness programs.
Maintain databases and document compliance activities.
Prepare or assist in the preparation of applications for necessary permits.
Review or evaluate project proposals, designs for treatment or disposal facilities, and preparations for cleanup or remediation projects.
Manage and oversee site assessments, operational budgets, and the maintenance of operations records for wind field activities.
Train employees in operations, safety, environmental issues, and coordinate their training in remediation or cleanup procedures.
Provide technical support, manage warranty services, and oversee subcontractors or consultants to maintain quality and conformance.
Prepare requests for proposals and manage the negotiation or approval of contracts related to wind farm operations.
Establish goals for wind field operations and develop processes for transitioning operations from construction to commercial stages.
Identify sources of environmental contamination, conduct risk assessments, and design remediation plans to improve environmental conditions.
Direct the implementation of policies and procedures for compliance with environmental standards across the organization.
Coordinate hazardous waste disposal, develop risk management strategies, and maintain records of environmental projects and compliance.
Estimate costs for environmental cleanup, prepare inspection reports on site progress, and manage relationships with stakeholders involved.
